# The Old Clockmaker's Detour

They did not use his name in Whitmoor. "The clockmaker is here," the miller's
wife called down the lane, and doors opened along the row. The old clockmaker
worked through the afternoon on a music box that had not sung in a decade,
and left before anyone thought to ask where he was headed.

Only the miller's daughter noticed the cedar case, and the careful way he
carried it, as if it held something more than tools.
